entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary:
The discussion on the relationship between block size and transaction fees in blockchain technology emphasizes that this relationship is not linear. In environments where block sizes are restricted, transaction fees tend to be higher. This scenario favors transactions with larger amounts of satoshis (sats) as they compete for priority processing, leading to a situation where those willing to pay higher fees secure the top spots in block space allocation.
Smaller block sizes are argued to enhance the security of the network. This security comes from two main aspects: the process of validation and the economic model of fees. When block sizes are small, there is no need for a competitive bidding war over block space, allowing transactions to be posted at minimal cost (e.g., 1 SAT/byte). Conversely, if a bidding war ensues due to limited space and high demand, larger transactions will face substantially higher fees. This dynamic has been supported by various academic papers and can be demonstrated through simple examples crafted to illustrate the effects of block size on transaction fees and network security.
